lastReleaseMessage: preset recall filters dangling custom-section keys; spec sec5+sec8 documented

barRaise.topFinding: The frozen streak broke in the right direction. After eight days at 3d9b480, v0.18.5 (249891b) landed 2026-06-22 with real app code (the preset-recall custom-key filter in src/ui/editor.js), a new section-8 locked decision, and a pill bump agreeing across all three sources. The same commit cleared three reprinted findings: the uncommitted PROJECT_SPEC.md edit is committed, the tree is clean, the stale .git/index.lock is gone, and every apothecary item in the YaE queue is now terminal, including the payload-less stale-spec ticket flagged as noise yesterday. Holding the verdict at stalled forced the cleanup instead of a tenth reprint. Two decide-not-fix items survive, both MEDIUM: the 864 MB .git is still undecided, and v1.0 is still uncut at 98 percent with daily cadence attached. Neither is an emergency now that the project is moving.

actions.title: Decide and close the 864 MB `.git` bloat: `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 5M` + force-push, or a "Won't fix" locked decision in PROJECT_SPEC section 8. The one carry-over that did not move this cycle.
Cut v1.0, mark "Symbol + label polish" done, and move Apothecary to weekly (constellation-only) review. Downgraded from HIGH; the cleanup pass this cycle is the natural stopping point.
